summary: This notebook provides a baseline pipeline for a multi-target commodity prediction competition, demonstrating how to efficiently handle 424 separate regression tasks. It trains independent LightGBM models per target while sharing a common feature set, using a target-index encoding trick to unify the training loop. The author implements a custom RankCorr-Sharpe cross-validation metric to align with the competition's scoring methodology. Readers learn how to structure multi-output regression workflows, manage type conversions for tabular data, and integrate custom evaluation metrics into a Kaggle inference server. The approach prioritizes simplicity and reproducibility over complex model architectures.
competition_flow: Reads train/test CSVs via Polars, trains 424 independent LightGBM regressors with a target-index feature and basic type casting, evaluates them using a custom RankCorr-Sharpe metric via TimeSeriesSplit, and exports a Kaggle-compatible inference server for prediction generation.
data_reading: polars.read_csv for train.csv, test.csv, and train_labels.csv
data_processing: Casts object-type columns to numeric via pd.to_numeric(errors='coerce')
Converts categorical columns to integer codes
Injects a constant target_name_encoded column mapping each target to its index
insights: Training separate regressors per target with a shared feature set and a target-index encoding allows efficient multi-output prediction without complex multi-task architectures.
A custom RankCorr-Sharpe metric effectively captures both ranking accuracy and consistency across rows, aligning with competition scoring requirements.
Using Polars for data I/O and pandas for in-memory processing provides a practical hybrid workflow for large tabular datasets.
